text.title: Google Ads Reporting and Optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real ads-google-reporting engagements. Each pairs a symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Report headline metric is CTR or impressions; CPA footnoted or absent.
antipattern.root-cause: Author optimized on a vanity metric because conversion data was harder to pull.
antipattern.fix: Always lead with CPA + ROAS + conversion count; CTR / impressions move to diagnostic appendix.

antipattern.symptom: Aggregate looks healthy; mobile bleeds while desktop subsidizes.
antipattern.root-cause: Decisions made on campaign-level averages only.
antipattern.fix: Segment by device, network, location, audience, time before any bid or budget call.

antipattern.symptom: Spend through irrelevant searches creeps up week-over-week; ROAS drifts down.
antipattern.root-cause: Search Terms report skipped due to time pressure.
antipattern.fix: Block 10 min weekly for cost-descending Search Terms triage; add negatives same session.
